text: The sun was shining brightly as sisters Samaira and Prisha hopped into the back of the car. "Are we there yet?" Prisha giggled, swinging her legs with excitement. Their mom, Shweta, buckled them into their seats. They were heading to the big city mall for a day full of surprises, and the sisters couldn't wait to start their adventure.

The mall was even bigger than they remembered! Shweta held Samaira’s hand as they walked through the giant glass doors. Everything sparkled under the bright lights, and the sound of happy music filled the air. "Let's start our adventure," Shweta said with a wink as they stepped onto the polished, shiny floors.

All that walking made their tummies rumble. "Burger party time!" shouted Samaira. They found a cozy booth at the food court. Prisha and Samaira shared a plate of golden fries and juicy burgers. Each bite was delicious, and the sisters couldn't stop laughing as they dipped their fries in a big puddle of red ketchup.

After lunch, it was time to explore the shops. Shweta took Prisha to see the colorful clothes and shiny accessories. They found a rack of sparkly hats and silly glasses. Shweta held up a bright pink scarf, and Prisha twirled around, feeling like a princess in the middle of the store.

Suddenly, while they were passing a large indoor fountain, Samaira stopped in her tracks. "Look! It's Aunt Saka!" she cried. Running toward them was their favorite aunt. Saka was wearing a giant grin. "Saka! What are you doing here?" Samaira asked, giving her a big high-five.

Saka reached into her bag and pulled out a small, brightly wrapped box. "I have a surprise for my favorite sisters!" she said. Prisha reached out to take the gift, her eyes wide with wonder. Inside were two beautiful, handmade friendship bracelets for the girls to wear so they could have a special memory of the day.

"Why don't we all go on a ride together?" Shweta suggested. Saka’s eyes lit up. All four of them walked toward the station to catch the Metro rail. Shweta and Saka led the way, chatting happily about their lucky meeting. The two women walked side-by-side, sharing stories as they followed the signs to the platform.

The Metro train arrived with a soft "whoosh!" Samaira and Saka found a seat together right by the big window. As the train started to move, the buildings outside looked like they were racing past. "We're flying on the rails!" Samaira cheered, pressing her nose against the cool glass.

Across the aisle, Shweta and Prisha were watching the city too. Prisha pointed at the tall skyscrapers and the twinkling lights. "Look, Mommy! Everything looks so small from up here!" she exclaimed. Shweta hugged her close, enjoying the rhythmic movement of the train as it sped through the city.

The train ride was so much fun, but the day wasn't over yet. Shweta led them to a colorful shop with a giant ice cream cone on the sign. "An ice cream party for my favorite explorers," she said. Samaira jumped for joy as she looked at all the different flavors behind the glass.

Saka and Prisha picked out their favorite toppings. Saka chose colorful sprinkles, and Prisha wanted extra chocolate chips. They laughed as the shopkeeper piled the scoops high on their cones. The sweet smell of sugar and cream filled the air, making everyone’s mouth water.

Samaira and Prisha sat on a bench, each holding a cone with two huge scoops. It was cold, sweet, and the perfect end to their big day. They had shopped, eaten burgers, met Aunt Saka, and ridden the Metro. Truly, it was the best day ever for the two sisters!
